lesson_title: Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator
objective: Compare two fractions with the same numerator by reasoning about the size of the parts

pretest.question: Ava has 2/4 of a ribbon. Zoe has 2/6 of the same ribbon. Who has more?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 같은 분자 규칙 — 분모 작은 쪽이 큼
learn.content: When two fractions have the SAME NUMERATOR, you have the same number of pieces. But the pieces are different sizes! A smaller denominator means each piece is BIGGER. Think: splitting a pizza into 3 slices → big slices. Splitting into 8 slices → small slices. Example: 2/3 vs 2/8 → 2 big slices vs 2 small slices → 2/3 > 2/8. 예: Which is greater: 1/2 or 1/6? → 1/2. (Same numerator (1). Halves are bigger than sixths → 1/2 > 1/6.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분수 스트립 — 같은 조각 수, 다른 크기
learn.content: Use fraction strips to see this. Two bars, same length. Bar A split into 4 parts → each part is big. Bar B split into 8 parts → each part is small. If you shade 3 parts on each: 3/4 is clearly more area than 3/8. 예: Look at two fraction strips. Both have 2 parts shaded. One is split into 3 parts, the other into 6 parts. Which shows the greater fraction? → 2/3 (the strip split into 3 parts). (Fewer parts = bigger pieces. 2/3 > 2/6.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 조각 크기 비교 — 사분의 vs 팔분의
learn.content: Remember: SAME numerator → look at denominators. SMALLER denominator = BIGGER pieces = GREATER fraction. This is the opposite of what you might expect! 예: Compare: 4/8 ○ 4/4 → 4/4 is greater (4/8 < 4/4). (Same numerator (4). Fourths are bigger pieces than eighths → 4/4 > 4/8.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 분모 ↔ 조각 크기 — 반비례 관계
learn.content: 분모 b는 '몇 등분?'을 의미. b가 클수록 한 조각이 작음.   분모 2 → 한 조각 = 1/2 (큼)   분모 4 → 한 조각 = 1/4   분모 8 → 한 조각 = 1/8 (작음) 🔍 핵심: 분모 ↑ → 조각 크기 ↓. 같은 분자라면 큰 조각이 모인 쪽이 큼.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 같은 분자 비교 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 같은 분자 분수 비교 3단계:   단계 1) 분자가 같은지 확인.   단계 2) 같다면 → 분모만 비교.   단계 3) 분모 작은 쪽이 큰 분수. 예: 3/4 vs 3/6 → 1) 분자 3 같음 ✓, 2) 4 vs 6, 3) 4 < 6 → 3/4 > 3/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 여러 분수 정렬 — 분모 큰 순 = 작은 분수 순
learn.content: 같은 분자 분수 정렬: 분모만 보면 됨. 예: 2/3, 2/8, 2/4 → 분모: 3, 8, 4. 큰 분수 순 = 분모 작은 순 → 2/3 > 2/4 > 2/8. 🔍 단축: 분모 정렬 후 분수 순서는 거꾸로.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 흔한 실수 — '분모 큰 = 분수 큰' 함정
learn.content: 흔한 실수 (M02): 1/8 > 1/3 because 8 > 3? ❌ 왜 틀렸나: 분모는 '나눈 칸 수'. 칸 수가 많으면 한 칸은 작음. 올바른: 분자 같으면 분모 작은 쪽이 큰 분수. 1/3 > 1/8. 🔍 직관 점검: 8조각 케이크의 1조각 vs 3조각 케이크의 1조각 — 어느 게 큼?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 실생활 예 — 피자 조각 비교
learn.content: 분수 비교는 실생활 직관과 일치:   '피자 4조각 중 3조각' (3/4) vs '피자 8조각 중 3조각' (3/8).   같은 피자, 4조각으로 나누면 한 조각이 큼 → 3/4가 더 많음. 🔍 같은 개수의 조각이라도 조각 크기가 다르면 양이 다름.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Compare: 3/6 ○ 3/4
try.hint: Same numerator (3). Which denominator gives bigger pieces?
guided_steps: Step 3: Fourths are bigger than sixths.

try.question: Two same-size water bottles. Liam drank 2/3 of his. Nora drank 2/8 of hers. Who drank more water?
try.hint: Same numerator (2). Compare the size of the pieces.
guided_steps: Step 3: Thirds are bigger than eighths.

practice_r3.question: Yuna says 1/8 > 1/3 because 8 > 3. What would you tell Yuna?
practice_r3.answer: The denominator tells the size of each piece, not how much you have. Bigger denominator = smaller pieces. Since eighths are smaller than thirds, 1/8 < 1/3.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Without drawing a picture, explain why 4/6 > 4/8.
practice_r3.answer: Same numerator (4). Sixths are bigger pieces than eighths because the whole is split into fewer parts. 4 bigger pieces > 4 smaller pieces, so 4/6 > 4/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.3 'Compare Fractions with the Same Numerator' pp.361-364
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions

practice_r3.question: If 2/3 = 4/6 (equivalent), what does that imply about comparing 2/3 and 2/6?
choices: A. 2/3 > 2/6 (4 조각 vs 2 조각, 같은 단위)
